params.name: Echo and Narcissus
params.description:
On Jul 1, 1895 AD, Waterhouse painted this masterpiece.
Echo and Narcissus is the work of the English painter John William Waterhouse. It illustrates the myth of the nymph Echo, who could only repeat the words of others, and Narcissus, who fell in love with his own reflection in a pool of water. Echo fell in love with Narcissus, but he rejected her. In the end, Echo was heartbroken and spent the rest of her life in lonely glens until nothing but an echo sound remained of her, while Narcissus …
